description: Tokenizer-specific English-only routing vocabulary variant based on the 86-d semantic template. All semantic/pragmatic dimensions are preserved; tokenizer-backed surface dimensions were regenerated for sp2048.
intended_use: Sparse lexical/class cue routing for attention-less mixture-of-experts systems, megakernel routers, bag-of-words gates, hybrid token-and-class routers, and conversation/task triage.

normalization.label_format: snake_case_preferred; tokenizer-backed surface forms are lowercased and normalized by replacing spaces, hyphens, and apostrophes with underscores
normalization.orthography_policy: American and British English variants are included; where spellings differ, paired forms are kept adjacent inside `english_variant` and preserved where useful in other dimensions.
design_principles: Preserve the original 8 semantic dimensions for backward compatibility.
Add orthogonal routing dimensions for intent, discourse, output format, stack, modality, safety, compliance, and audience.
Favor routing-relevant classes and surface cues over narrow exemplars.
Allow controlled redundancy across dimensions when it improves recall for sparse or attention-less routing.
Keep terms broad-to-mid specificity so dimensions generalize across unseen conversations.
Include English-only locale and orthography coverage rather than multilingual translation cues.
normalization.tokenizer_backfill_normalization: strip leading whitespace from tokenizer entry, lowercase, retain English-only ASCII words, replace apostrophes/hyphens with underscores
normalization.tokenizer_backfill_scope: English-only single-word tokenizer entries that add routing value; subword fragments and non-English items excluded

dimension_descriptions.entity_type: Core entity classes covering people, groups, institutions, documents, digital artifacts, biological entities, places, events, and abstract objects.
dimension_descriptions.entity_role: Semantic roles and participant roles such as requester, responder, owner, attacker, buyer, teacher, and reviewer.
dimension_descriptions.named_entity_pattern: Placeholder classes for proper names and typed named entities such as person names, company names, law names, and tickers.
dimension_descriptions.action: Verbs and operations across physical acts, cognition, communication, governance, commerce, medicine, and software/ML workflows.
dimension_descriptions.event_type: Common event categories spanning conversations, business processes, politics, computing incidents, health events, travel, and disasters.
dimension_descriptions.property: Descriptive attributes including physical, logical, legal, ethical, statistical, operational, and formatting properties.
dimension_descriptions.state_condition: Status or lifecycle states such as active, pending, blocked, healthy, authenticated, deployed, and resolved.
dimension_descriptions.emotion_sentiment: Affect, sentiment, stress, motivation, and interpersonal emotional states useful for routing tone-sensitive conversations.
dimension_descriptions.need_preference: Goals, wants, requirements, priorities, tolerances, and stylistic or practical preferences expressed by a user.
dimension_descriptions.domain: Broad subject-matter coverage across sciences, engineering, humanities, business, policy, health, and everyday life topics.
dimension_descriptions.industry_vertical: Industry and sector cues such as banking, healthcare, SaaS, telecom, manufacturing, travel, and public sector.
dimension_descriptions.temporal: Time expressions, scheduling cues, eras, cadence, lifecycle timing, and relative-time language.
dimension_descriptions.spatial_relation: Relative location, direction, physical arrangement, site context, and system-topology position cues.
dimension_descriptions.geography_locale: Geographic and market-locality cues, including US/UK locale distinctions and broader regional references.
dimension_descriptions.causality: Cause-effect language, mechanisms, drivers, confounders, feedback loops, interventions, and failure propagation concepts.
dimension_descriptions.intent: User goals and task intents such as explain, compare, debug, draft, summarize, calculate, research, and advise.
dimension_descriptions.dialogue_act: Conversational speech acts such as greeting, objection, suggestion, apology, clarification, warning, and summary.
dimension_descriptions.question_type: Interrogative patterns such as definitional, causal, troubleshooting, planning, yes/no, and hypothetical questions.
dimension_descriptions.answer_format: Requested output shapes such as table, JSON, memo, proof, code block, checklist, timeline, or email.
dimension_descriptions.reasoning_mode: Analytical modes such as deductive, causal, first-principles, scenario, debugging, legal, and statistical reasoning.
dimension_descriptions.discourse_relation: Rhetorical and structural relations such as background, contrast, evidence, example, concession, and conclusion.
dimension_descriptions.epistemic_stance: Certainty, uncertainty, verification status, evidential strength, and assumption sensitivity cues.
dimension_descriptions.evidence_source: Source-type cues such as official docs, papers, logs, screenshots, reports, filings, and firsthand evidence.
dimension_descriptions.social_register: Register, genre, and communicative setting cues such as legalese, support chat, boardroom, academic, or meme style.
dimension_descriptions.tone_style: Surface tone signals such as friendly, blunt, diplomatic, playful, urgent, skeptical, or authoritative.
dimension_descriptions.politeness_strategy: Politeness markers and face-management language such as please, sorry, thank you, kindly, and gentle reminders.
dimension_descriptions.rhetorical_device: Persuasive or literary devices such as metaphor, analogy, hyperbole, satire, framing, and reductio.
dimension_descriptions.narrative_pov: Point-of-view and narration settings such as first person, omniscient, epistolary, or imperative mood.
dimension_descriptions.audience_type: Target audience classes such as layperson, executive, expert, student, regulator, child, or international English reader.
dimension_descriptions.expertise_level: Knowledge-depth cues from no background through doctoral or specialist level.
dimension_descriptions.accessibility_need: Accessibility and readability needs such as easy read, captions, alt text, dyslexia-friendly layout, or simple sentences.
dimension_descriptions.modality: Content modality cues such as text, image, audio, code, spreadsheet, video, PDF, or multimodal input.
dimension_descriptions.channel_medium: Delivery medium cues such as email, chat, forum, documentation, meeting, webinar, phone call, or ticket system.
dimension_descriptions.document_type: Document classes such as report, spec, proposal, contract, postmortem, resume, script, or prompt.
dimension_descriptions.document_section: Section-level cues such as abstract, background, methodology, risks, appendix, recommendations, or conclusion.
dimension_descriptions.data_type: Data-shape cues such as time series, tabular, graph, embedding, telemetry, logs, JSON, or geospatial data.
dimension_descriptions.file_format: File extension and serialization cues spanning text, office formats, code files, multimedia, archives, and model artifacts.
dimension_descriptions.surface_pattern: Token-shape and formatting cues such as email addresses, timestamps, code fences, UUIDs, hashtags, or semantic versions.
dimension_descriptions.programming_language: Programming-language cues covering general-purpose, scripting, scientific, systems, and web languages.
dimension_descriptions.query_language: Query and selection languages such as SQL, GraphQL, XPath, PromQL, KQL, or regex.
dimension_descriptions.framework_library: Framework, library, and infrastructure cues across ML, data, web, infra, testing, and LLM serving stacks.
dimension_descriptions.platform_environment: Runtime and deployment environment cues such as browser, mobile app, cloud, edge, HPC cluster, or serverless.
dimension_descriptions.operating_system: Operating-system and distro cues such as Windows, macOS, Ubuntu, Android, or WSL.
dimension_descriptions.device_type: Hardware/device cues such as phone, laptop, server, router, VR headset, kiosk, or payment terminal.
dimension_descriptions.ai_ml_subdomain: AI/ML system cues including MoE, routing, quantization, decoding, kernels, alignment, and evaluation topics.
dimension_descriptions.math_operation: Mathematical task cues such as integration, proof, factorization, optimization, matrix algebra, or shortest path.
dimension_descriptions.statistical_task: Statistics and ML evaluation tasks such as regression, hypothesis testing, clustering, forecasting, or causal inference.
dimension_descriptions.business_function: Organizational functions such as product, sales, finance, support, procurement, analytics, or investor relations.
dimension_descriptions.project_phase: Project lifecycle phases from ideation and discovery through rollout, maintenance, deprecation, and retirement.
dimension_descriptions.software_lifecycle: Software delivery lifecycle stages such as design, implementation, testing, canary release, monitoring, and hotfixing.
dimension_descriptions.support_issue_type: Customer or internal support issue types such as billing, login, outage, abuse report, or accessibility issue.
dimension_descriptions.commerce_stage: Commercial funnel and account lifecycle stages from awareness and checkout through renewal, churn, and winback.
dimension_descriptions.life_context: Everyday life contexts such as career, parenting, dating, immigration, budgeting, health, hobbies, and grief support.
dimension_descriptions.interpersonal_relation: Relationship cues such as colleague, manager, landlord-tenant, doctor-patient, or parent-child.
dimension_descriptions.demographic_attribute: Broad demographic and situational user descriptors useful for audience-sensitive routing.
dimension_descriptions.safety_risk: Safety-policy and harm-risk cues such as violence, self-harm, fraud, malware, medical, or child-safety risk.
dimension_descriptions.privacy_sensitivity: Sensitivity levels and data classes such as PII, health data, secrets, trade secrets, or legal privilege.
dimension_descriptions.compliance_framework: Compliance and governance cues such as GDPR, HIPAA, SOC 2, WCAG, copyright, or KYC/AML.
dimension_descriptions.constraint_type: Hard or soft constraints such as budget, latency, brevity, evidence requirements, locale, or no-code instructions.
dimension_descriptions.optimization_target: Optimization objectives such as accuracy, latency, cost, safety, fairness, interpretability, or readability.
dimension_descriptions.metric_kpi: Evaluation metrics and business KPIs such as MRR, churn, p95 latency, F1, MTTR, or NPS.
dimension_descriptions.unit_currency_measure: Measurement units and currency cues including SI, imperial, time, storage, temperature, and major currencies.
dimension_descriptions.numeric_expression: Magnitude and numeric-language cues such as percentages, ranges, YoY growth, ballpark estimates, and quartiles.
dimension_descriptions.english_variant: English-only orthography and lexical-variant cues, with American and British forms kept adjacent where they differ.
dimension_descriptions.discourse_marker: Conversational discourse markers such as however, anyway, in fact, by the way, or to sum up.
dimension_descriptions.deictic_reference: Context-indexing words such as this, that, above, previous, attached file, or current thread.
dimension_descriptions.logical_operator: Logic and connective cues such as and, or, if, only if, at least, subset, or equal to.
dimension_descriptions.instruction_pattern: Prompt-shaping instructions such as be concise, make a table, use examples, no bullets, or use US English.
dimension_descriptions.conversation_phase: Conversation-stage cues such as context gathering, exploration, deep dive, revision, or closing.
dimension_descriptions.culture_reference: Cultural and seasonal reference cues such as Christmas, Thanksgiving, Black Friday, graduation, or bank holiday.
dimension_descriptions.creative_genre: Creative-genre cues such as fantasy, noir, satire, memoir, documentary, or science fiction.
dimension_descriptions.code_artifact: Software artifact classes such as function, API endpoint, migration, CI pipeline, checkpoint, or router config.
dimension_descriptions.math_object: Mathematical object cues such as vectors, tensors, sets, distributions, theorems, or manifolds.
dimension_descriptions.workflow_artifact: Project-management and operations artifacts such as ticket, milestone, sprint, priority, or action item.
dimension_descriptions.argument_component: Argument-structure cues such as claim, premise, rebuttal, supporting point, or recommendation.
dimension_descriptions.social_context: Interaction setting cues such as professional, personal, therapy, interview, public, or confidential context.
dimension_descriptions.context_scope: Where the answer should ground itself: current turn, conversation history, attachment, web context, or real-world context.
dimension_descriptions.stance_position: Attitudinal stance cues such as supportive, opposed, approving, cautious, or ambivalent.
dimension_descriptions.scientific_method: Research-method cues such as hypothesis, randomization, replication, ablation, survey, or ethnography.
dimension_descriptions.law_policy_type: Legal and policy instrument types such as statute, regulation, bylaw, privacy policy, or executive order.
dimension_descriptions.negation_modality: Modal and normative cues such as must, may, should not, prohibited, permitted, or optional.
dimension_descriptions.pronoun_form: Raw English pronoun and pronoun-like surface forms absent from the original ontology, added from tokenizer comparison.
dimension_descriptions.function_word: High-value English grammar/function words absent from the original ontology, including articles, prepositions, auxiliaries, and connectives.
dimension_descriptions.contraction_surface: Normalized English contraction surface forms derived from tokenizer coverage, using underscores in place of apostrophes.
dimension_descriptions.chat_slang_surface: English chat/internet/slang surface tokens useful for conversational routing, including fillers, colloquialisms, and emphatic slang.
dimension_descriptions.common_surface_lexeme: Tokenizer-backed common English content-word backfill for sparse lexical routing; includes frequent everyday nouns, verbs, adjectives, names, and places absent from the original ontology.

tokenizer_variant.name: sp2048
tokenizer_variant.source_note: Locally trained SentencePiece unigram model on a synthetic English corpus biased toward the router ontology.
